name: Perfect Crewlink overlay and voice correctness

description: baseline script crashed because POSIX tail unavailable in harness shell

asi.hypothesis: establish baseline static bug score before code fixes
asi.rollback_reason: benchmark harness used tail; no product code tested
asi.next_action_hint: replace tail with Node output truncation or omit log tail, then rerun baseline

name: Perfect Crewlink overlay and voice correctness

description: baseline static bug score before fixes

asi.hypothesis: baseline captures six root-cause regressions plus existing typecheck failure
baseline_static_failures: AleLudu row count uses renderPlayers length
lobby rejoin passes clientId as playerId
VAD can overwrite active socket mapping
asi.typecheck_failure: ISettings lacks debugMode while Settings/SettingsStore use it

description: all targeted bug checks passed but edit hook reformatted huge unrelated surface

asi.hypothesis: fix all six bug root causes plus ISettings debugMode type error
asi.learned: logic changes worked and typecheck passed, but edit hook rewrote large TS files and touched src-tauri/src/assets.rs/get_host_platform formatting
asi.rollback_reason: secondary code-churn risk too high for surgical bugfix despite primary metric improvement
asi.next_action_hint: reapply same fixes with raw byte-preserving patching, then rerun metric

description: fix overlay Alt-Tab visibility, stable meeting slots, peer mapping, and top-down audio axes

asi.hypothesis: requested bugs share six root causes: focus-based overlay hiding, meeting slot reshuffle, stale socket updates, wrong reconnect player id, vertical-axis panning, and missing debugMode type
asi.overlay_fix: Windows overlay now hides only when Among Us is minimized or absent, not when another app has focus
asi.meeting_fix: Meeting slots now use one frozen order ref for every HUD mode and AleLudu row count uses frozen slot count
asi.voice_fix: VAD from stale sockets cannot override active client mapping; lobby rejoin sends player id; PannerNode uses x/z top-down axes with y fixed at 0
asi.validation: autoresearch.sh static checks pass and npm typecheck passes

name: Perfect Crewlink meeting roster growth correctness

description: baseline for roster-growth meeting freeze check

asi.hypothesis: a meeting can render before full roster arrives, so freezing on first partial state can still misalign later talking highlights
asi.new_check: benchmark now requires frozenMeetingOrderRef to refresh while src.length grows, but not shrink when players disappear mid-meeting

description: allow frozen meeting order to grow during initial roster load

asi.hypothesis: if first discussion frame has partial players, one-shot freeze can append late players at wrong slots; recompute only while roster length increases
asi.meeting_fix: frozenMeetingOrderRef now updates when src.length grows, but remains frozen for same-length death updates and shrink/removal events
asi.validation: static checks and typecheck pass

name: Perfect Crewlink duplicate socket mapping correctness

description: baseline for duplicate client socket mapping check

asi.hypothesis: if server briefly reports duplicate socket IDs for one client, last-write mapping can bind a player's position to the wrong audio stream
asi.new_check: benchmark now requires explicit duplicate-client socket preference and skipping non-selected duplicate sockets

description: dedupe duplicate client socket mappings by preferring active/previous sockets

asi.hypothesis: duplicate socket entries for one client can make audioElements for one peer get panned using another player's coordinates
asi.voice_fix: setClientConnectionMap now picks one socket per client, preferring active audio/peer connections and then previous socket, and skips non-selected duplicates
asi.validation: static checks and typecheck pass

name: Perfect Crewlink TypeScript and Rust checks

description: add Rust cargo check to autoresearch validation baseline

asi.hypothesis: Rust overlay changes need compiler coverage, not just TypeScript/static checks
asi.validation: cargo check and npm typecheck both pass on current fixes
asi.next_action_hint: future Rust overlay z-order changes will be caught by benchmark

name: Perfect Crewlink voice connect identity correctness

description: baseline for voice connect identity refresh checks

asi.hypothesis: initial lobby join can happen before myPlayer id is known; if the effect does not rerun and same-lobby connect does not refresh id, server can keep stale player identity
new_checks: same-lobby connect emits id with current player/client identity
connect effect dependency list tracks myPlayer id and gameState clientId

description: refresh voice server identity when player id becomes available

asi.hypothesis: server identity can remain stale after early same-lobby connect; stale identities can cascade into wrong peer/player mapping and spatial audio
asi.voice_fix: same-lobby connect now emits current id when playerId/clientId are valid; connect effect reruns when myPlayer id or clientId changes
asi.validation: static checks, npm typecheck, and cargo check pass

name: Perfect Crewlink dead-state overlay correctness

description: baseline for otherDead live player update check

asi.hypothesis: otherDead only updates on gameState transitions; mid-meeting deaths/disconnects can leave overlay/voice dead metadata stale and contribute to wrong highlight/sort behavior
asi.new_check: Voice dead-state effect must depend on gameState.players and avoid re-render churn with changed guard

description: keep otherDead synchronized with live player updates

asi.hypothesis: stale dead/disconnect state can make overlays sort/filter/highlight players incorrectly during meetings
asi.voice_fix: otherDead effect now tracks gameState.players, removes vanished clients, and returns old state when nothing changed to avoid churn
asi.validation: static checks, npm typecheck, and cargo check pass

name: Perfect Crewlink camera audio guard correctness

description: baseline for missing camera-position guard

asi.hypothesis: hear-through-cameras can index a missing camera position on maps/mods without camera metadata, crashing or leaving positional audio wrong
asi.new_check: calculateVoiceAudio must use safe cameras lookup and bail when selected camera position is missing

description: guard proximity camera audio against missing camera metadata

asi.hypothesis: missing camera metadata can crash or misposition hear-through-cameras audio, especially on modded maps
asi.voice_fix: calculateVoiceAudio now uses safe camera map lookup, returns silence for missing selected camera, and reuses safe camera collection for Skeld nearest-camera search
asi.validation: static checks, npm typecheck, and cargo check pass

name: Perfect Crewlink full build validation

description: add full Vite build to validation baseline

asi.hypothesis: fixes should pass production frontend build, not only typecheck/static checks
asi.validation: npm run build, npm run typecheck, cargo check, and static checks all pass

name: Perfect Crewlink talking highlight stale VAD guard

description: baseline for stale VAD talking highlight guard

asi.hypothesis: server VAD true can outlive audible remote audio and keep wrong meeting/player highlight active
asi.new_check: talking state should combine VAD with recent audio monitor activity via REMOTE_AUDIO_TALKING_GRACE_MS/serverVadTalking

description: gate remote talking highlights with recent audio activity

asi.hypothesis: stale server VAD can keep the wrong player highlighted even after audio has gone silent
asi.voice_fix: talking state now uses recent audio activity window for VAD-backed highlights while still allowing first VAD before monitor state exists
asi.validation: static checks, npm typecheck, npm build, and cargo check pass

name: Perfect Crewlink overlay hidden visibility refresh

description: baseline for overlay visibilitychange hidden-state refresh

asi.hypothesis: if the overlay webview visibilityState becomes hidden during Alt-Tab, visible-only visibilitychange handlers can miss resize/bootstrap refreshes
asi.new_check: Overlay.tsx should refresh size/init on every visibilitychange, not only when document.visibilityState is visible

description: refresh overlay size and bootstrap on every visibilitychange

asi.hypothesis: Alt-Tab can make overlay webview hidden without native window hiding; visible-gated handlers delayed state/size refresh until focus returned
asi.overlay_fix: visibilitychange now always refreshes window size and requests main-window bootstrap state
asi.validation: static checks, npm typecheck, npm build, and cargo check pass

description: attempted custom lint command blocked by autoresearch harness

name: Perfect Crewlink meeting roster append stability

description: baseline for meeting roster-growth append stability

asi.hypothesis: recomputing the frozen meeting order when roster length grows can reshuffle existing slots if death/disconnect state changed before the full roster arrived
asi.review_finding: reviewer flagged Overlay.tsx roster-growth recompute as medium severity
asi.new_check: frozen meeting order growth should append missing players without reordering existing frozen IDs

asi.meeting_fix: extracted sortedMeetingPlayerIds and appendMissingMeetingPlayers; existing frozen ids remain in order and only new ids append

asi.review_finding: reviewer flagged missing placeholders for old/non-AleLudu HUD as medium severity

asi.meeting_fix: overlaySlots now include Player|null entries; old/non-AleLudu HUD renders meetingPlaceholder divs for missing frozen ids while AleLudu keeps absolute positioning

name: Perfect Crewlink talking state disconnect correctness

new_checks: loop must clear tempTalking when audio is missing

description: baseline for unused overlay foreground state cleanup

name: Perfect Crewlink meeting same-length replacement slots

description: baseline for same-length meeting replacement slots

asi.review_finding: reviewer flagged same-length replacement as low severity

name: Perfect Crewlink embedded child overlay

description: baseline for embedding overlay as Among Us child window

asi.hypothesis: top-level always-on-top overlay covers taskbar and appears outside Among Us; embedding as child window should clip it to Among Us client area
asi.user_feedback: overlay shows outside Among Us and hides/covers taskbar until app closes
asi.new_check: Rust overlay code must use SetParent, WS_CHILD, and GetClientRect

description: embed overlay as child of Among Us client area

asi.hypothesis: top-level overlay caused taskbar cover/disappear and visible overlay outside game; child-window overlay should be clipped to Among Us client area
asi.overlay_fix: Windows overlay now uses GetClientRect, SetParent to Among Us HWND, WS_CHILD style, and SetWindowPos at client 0,0; detaches when disabled/missing/minimized
asi.validation: static checks, npm typecheck, npm build, and cargo check pass
asi.risk: cross-process SetParent can behave differently with exclusive fullscreen; test in windowed/borderless first

name: Perfect Crewlink non-topmost embedded overlay

description: baseline for removing topmost overlay flags after child embedding

asi.hypothesis: even after SetParent child embedding, overlay is still created/configured topmost and may race visible as a top-level taskbar-covering window
asi.user_feedback: taskbar disappeared while app was open
asi.new_check: overlay lib/config should not set topmost true once embedded child window is used

description: disable topmost flags for embedded overlay window

asi.hypothesis: removing always-on-top from embedded overlay prevents taskbar-covering races while child-parent embedding keeps overlay inside Among Us
asi.overlay_fix: configure_overlay_window sets always_on_top(false); tauri overlay window config alwaysOnTop=false
asi.validation: static checks, npm typecheck, npm build, and cargo check pass
asi.next_action_hint: rebuild installer/exe for testing; if overlay disappears behind exclusive fullscreen, test borderless/windowed or investigate DirectComposition/in-process overlay approaches

name: Perfect Crewlink child overlay z-order

asi.hypothesis: embedded overlay may be a child window but still render behind Among Us content unless moved to top of the child z-order
asi.next_action_hint: use HWND_TOP in SetWindowPos and remove SWP_NOZORDER for embedded overlay sizing

description: raise embedded overlay to top of Among Us child z-order

asi.overlay_fix: SetWindowPos now uses Some(HWND_TOP) and removes SWP_NOZORDER while keeping SWP_NOACTIVATE|SWP_FRAMECHANGED

name: Perfect Crewlink overlay detach visibility

asi.next_action_hint: add hide_overlay_window helper, call it on disabled/missing/minimized paths, and only force WS_VISIBLE for child embedding

description: attempt hide-before-detach but static check missed helper on CRLF files

name: Perfect Crewlink overlay detach visibility check fix

description: baseline with CRLF-safe overlay detach visibility check

asi.hypothesis: detach visibility root cause remains: overlay is detached while still styled visible, so it can flash as top-level window

description: hide overlay before detach and avoid forcing visible popup style

asi.hypothesis: hiding before detach and clearing WS_VISIBLE when detaching prevents embedded overlay from flashing as a top-level popup outside Among Us or over taskbar
asi.overlay_fix: added hide_overlay_window helper for disabled/missing/minimized paths; detach path no longer ORs WS_VISIBLE into popup style
asi.validation: static checks, npm typecheck, npm build, and cargo check pass
asi.next_action_hint: review SetParent style order next: Windows docs recommend setting WS_CHILD before SetParent when assigning a non-null parent

name: Perfect Crewlink SetParent child style order

description: baseline for SetParent child style order

asi.hypothesis: SetParent called before WS_CHILD/WS_POPUP style changes can create transient top-level overlay behavior or unreliable child embedding; Windows docs recommend setting WS_CHILD before assigning a parent
asi.new_check: set_overlay_child_styles must set WS_CHILD before SetParent for child embedding
asi.validation: existing overlay, meeting, voice, typecheck, build, and cargo checks pass except new style-order check
asi.next_action_hint: branch set_overlay_child_styles so parent Some sets child style before SetParent, while detach keeps hide-first behavior

description: set child window style before SetParent embedding

asi.hypothesis: setting WS_CHILD before SetParent avoids transient top-level overlay behavior and follows Windows SetParent style guidance for cross-process child embedding
asi.overlay_fix: set_overlay_child_styles now sets WS_CHILD/clears WS_POPUP before SetParent(Some(parent)); detach still hides first, then SetParent(None), then clears WS_CHILD/WS_VISIBLE
asi.validation: static checks, npm typecheck, npm build, and cargo check pass
asi.next_action_hint: consider applying SWP_FRAMECHANGED on detach style changes too, since current detach passes size=None and therefore does not call SetWindowPos after changing WS_CHILD/WS_POPUP

name: Perfect Crewlink detach framechanged style apply

description: baseline for detach SWP_FRAMECHANGED style apply

asi.hypothesis: detach changes WS_CHILD/WS_POPUP/WS_VISIBLE but passes size=None, so no SetWindowPos(SWP_FRAMECHANGED) runs to apply the frame/style changes immediately
asi.new_check: set_overlay_child_styles must call SetWindowPos with SWP_NOMOVE|SWP_NOSIZE|SWP_NOZORDER|SWP_NOACTIVATE|SWP_FRAMECHANGED when size is None
asi.validation: existing overlay, meeting, voice, typecheck, build, and cargo checks pass except new detach framechanged check
asi.next_action_hint: add size=None SetWindowPos path after detach style changes

description: attempt detach framechanged but z-order check overmatched detach flags

asi.hypothesis: calling SetWindowPos(SWP_FRAMECHANGED) on detach should apply WS_CHILD/WS_POPUP style changes immediately
asi.rollback_reason: existing child z-order static check overmatched the new detach-only SWP_NOZORDER flag sequence, so primary metric did not improve even though the intended new check passed
asi.next_action_hint: narrow overlay_child_z_order_top check to the size Some embedding SetWindowPos block, then rerun baseline and reapply detach framechanged fix
asi.validation: typecheck, build, and cargo check passed

name: Perfect Crewlink detach framechanged check narrowing

description: baseline with narrowed child z-order check

asi.hypothesis: detach framechanged root cause remains; benchmark z-order check should only reject SWP_NOZORDER in the size/embedding SetWindowPos block, not the detach-only framechanged path
asi.benchmark_fix: overlay_child_z_order_top now inspects only the if-let-size SetWindowPos block
asi.validation: baseline still fails only detach framechanged check
asi.next_action_hint: reapply detach SetWindowPos(SWP_FRAMECHANGED) path

description: apply framechanged after detach style updates

asi.hypothesis: after detaching and changing WS_CHILD/WS_POPUP/WS_VISIBLE, SetWindowPos(SWP_FRAMECHANGED) should run even without a resize so Windows applies the new top-level hidden style immediately
asi.overlay_fix: size=None path now calls SetWindowPos with SWP_NOMOVE|SWP_NOSIZE|SWP_NOZORDER|SWP_NOACTIVATE|SWP_FRAMECHANGED; embed size path still raises with HWND_TOP and no SWP_NOZORDER
asi.validation: static checks, npm typecheck, npm build, and cargo check pass
asi.next_action_hint: inspect whether detach path should preserve previous parent state to avoid redundant SetParent(None) calls, but only if runtime testing shows flicker

name: Perfect Crewlink redundant SetParent guard

description: baseline for skipping redundant SetParent calls

asi.hypothesis: refreshing overlay while already embedded repeatedly calls SetParent and can cause avoidable z-order/style churn or flicker during Alt-Tab/resizes
asi.new_check: set_overlay_child_styles should read current parent with GetParent and call SetParent only when parent changes; detach should skip SetParent(None) if already unparented
asi.validation: existing overlay, meeting, voice, typecheck, build, and cargo checks pass except new redundant SetParent check
asi.next_action_hint: import GetParent, compute current_parent once, guard SetParent(Some(parent_hwnd)) and SetParent(None)

description: skip redundant SetParent calls during overlay refresh

asi.hypothesis: avoiding repeated SetParent calls when overlay is already parented/unparented reduces z-order/style churn and flicker while keeping child embedding intact
asi.overlay_fix: set_overlay_child_styles reads current parent with GetParent; SetParent(Some(parent)) only runs when parent differs, and SetParent(None) only runs when a parent exists
asi.validation: static checks, npm typecheck, npm build, and cargo check pass
asi.next_action_hint: runtime-test Alt-Tab/window resize; if flicker persists, collect traces around refresh_overlay_window call frequency before adding more guards

name: Perfect Crewlink stale audio cleanup without peer connection

asi.new_check: disconnectPeer no-connection branch must call disconnectAudioElement(peer) before return
asi.validation: existing overlay, meeting, voice, typecheck, build, and cargo checks pass except new stale-audio cleanup check
asi.next_action_hint: call disconnectAudioElement(peer) in the !connection branch after client state cleanup and before return

asi.hypothesis: if the Peer object is already gone but audio elements/monitors remain, the no-connection branch should still tear down audio to prevent stale activity and wrong talking highlights
asi.voice_fix: disconnectPeer now calls disconnectAudioElement(peer) before returning from the !connection branch
asi.validation: static checks, npm typecheck, npm build, and cargo check pass
asi.next_action_hint: inspect preserveClientState duplicate-socket path for whether audio cleanup can clear active replacement state; current cleanup only clears monitor state when active socket is absent or equals peer

name: Perfect Crewlink VAD socket-mapped client identity

description: baseline for socket-mapped VAD client identity

asi.hypothesis: remaining meeting highlights can be wrong when VAD payload carries a stale client id for a socket; highlight state should use the locally mapped socket->client identity instead of trusting data.client.clientId

asi.validation: existing overlay, meeting, voice, typecheck, build, and cargo checks pass except new VAD identity check

description: map VAD activity through active socket client identity

asi.hypothesis: using locally known socket->client mapping for VAD prevents stale server payload client ids from lighting up a dead/wrong player card
asi.voice_fix: VAD handler now computes vadClientId from socketClientsRef.current[data.socketId]?.clientId ?? data.client.clientId and uses it for stale guard, otherVAD, and connection updates
asi.user_feedback_addressed: wrong highlights for subset of players, especially dead/wrong-color highlights
asi.validation: static checks, npm typecheck, npm build, and cargo check pass
asi.next_action_hint: build installer and test meeting highlights again; if bug remains, inspect overlay color/name mapping for shiftedColor/nameHash collisions

name: Perfect Crewlink audio monitor socket-mapped client identity

description: keep audio monitor activity on active socket client mapping

asi.voice_fix: startPeerAudioMonitor now re-reads socketClientsRef.current[peer]?.clientId, clears old client audio activity when mapping changes, updates monitor.clientId, and writes audio activity/connection state to monitor.clientId

name: Perfect Crewlink voice activity requires mapped sockets

new_checks: VAD handler must require socketClientsRef.current[data.socketId] before updating otherVAD

name: Perfect Crewlink audio smoothing simulation
